column7:
> braincels is filled with people with mental illnesses especially depression, who are struggling to cope. IncelTears is a place for relatively healthy people to make fun of them.

Have you seen the comments incel tears highlights? They're usually pedophilic and horrible and talk about wanting to kill or rape people.

I'm sorry but mentally ill people don't get a free pass. I'm actually offended on the behalf of mentally ill people that you hold them so low in your mind.

Please understand that r/IncelTears takes screenshots from various places. Separate websites outside Reddit like incels.me and especially truecels.org **allow** posts advocating rape, violence, and pedophilia. 

**Braincels doesn't allow those type of posts.** However, people often confuse all content on IncelTears as coming from Braincels, simply because it's the largest incel sub on Reddit.

@exact-terrace Wow, way to generalize an entire subreddit full of people who are already struggling. Did you just make up all the stuff about them being pedophiles and wanting to rape people? That’s not even a thing on IncelTears, you know. Maybe you should read the rules before spewing your braincel nonsense.

@tough-caulk Thanks for the fact check, but I guess you’re too busy being a brainwashed mod to notice how many people here are just here to stir the pot. Maybe take a break from your precious “rules” and see how many people actually care about your moral high ground.

@succulent-halibut I see you're concerned about generalizations and assumptions in the discussion. Could you share what specific rules or guidelines you think should be in place to prevent harmful generalizations in this subreddit?

column7: God may have "demonstrated his power" to you, but how does that make anything said in the Bible true?  The Bible is a book written by people.  Even if God is definitely real and omnipotent, that would in no way prove that the people who wrote the Bible weren't making everything they wrote up.

>The Bible is the word of God, which we know because God tells us this in the Bible.

That's not a fallacy.  "My research paper is my word, and we know this because I tell you so in my research paper".  Or, "My comment is the word of me, which we know because I tell you this in my comment."  It's mostly a statement of ipso facto.  The Word of God is true, the Bible spreads the Word of God (meaning his message; rarely does God speak directly in the Bible, and even then it's through someone like Moses or Jesus).  Therefore the Bible is the Word of God.

I'm not a practicing Catholic or religious at all.  One can easily find this to be silly or dumb or okay or kind of a good idea maybe, but that doesn't mean we need to paint it like some logical fallacy - especially when it's not asserting this premise at all.

God is an omnipotent being and all-knowing.  What he says is true regardless of a book's existence.  The Bible is a way for Christians to keep the Word of God alive and spread its message, as it was done for hundreds and thousands of years.

But the Bible isn't true due to circular reasoning.  That's something bad comedians and bitter agnostics come up with to make a joke.  Christian thinking here is very simple and straightforward, and one can easily disagree with many of its teachings, but to make a circular argument out of nothing is pointless.

When God spoke to Abraham, he didn't need a Bible.  He was God, and could perform things no one could.  Jesus was the same way.  The Bible was mankind's attempt to spread the Word of God, not be the only words of God ever.  Catholics still honor saints, showing that they believe miracles are being performed.  They're just very picky about them.  Non-Catholics continually pray to God and see events as being signs of his existence.  These can also be the actions or words of God.

To Christians, God has already demonstrated his power.  It would stand to reason that what he says is True with a capital T.  But if God came back to Earth and said some stuff in the Bible was no longer applicable, it would be done with.

column7:
OK, so you have no idea how courts can prove that.

That's because they can't. It's impossible. 

Unless a business owner is a dumbass and tells the person they're not being hired because they're gay and it's recorded, then they can prosecute. But that never happens. Basically all discrimination cases are thrown out due to lack of evidence.

Why have any laws then? Ideally people wouldn't do anything bad. Punishing people who want to do bad things doesn't work. They do it anyways. Oh look, now they are in jail and you haven't changed anyone's mind. Guess law is bad. 

It's not about punishing or changing the bad actors but giving recourse and protection to the vulnerable, injured party. Why should society be able to discriminate against you with no recourse? Why should our laws allow that?

Why do you have to change hearts and minds? Discrimination isn't just this unfortunate reality that the majority will get around to on its own time when it stops feeling attacked. It's an injustice and intolerable now and in the past and as long as it continues. 

Homophobic hiring practices are anti-American and there should be no tolerance for them in our society or legal framework.

@avocado-comment: Wow, this is such a heated debate. I think it's sad that so many people still don't get it about discrimination. If someone can't prove they were treated unfairly, that's a hard one to fight, but shouldn't we at least try to make things fair for everyone? I wish more people would just listen instead of doubling down on "well, that's how it's always been."
@avocado-comment: Your perspective is important—how do you think we can improve fairness without requiring absolute proof in every case?

column7:
I'm a guy - I completely get that individual people would not enjoy what she creates, but we have ways of examining what the general reaction to her work has been across a larger sample size. Your opinion is just as invalid as mine is!

All three seasons of her show on Metacritic score in the mid sixties. 3.5/5 stars, which is a decent rating. The audience evaluation of Trainwreck is similar - 66% on Rotten Tomatoes. 

People put down Schumer to the degree that we would expect her creative output to be of a similar calibre to modern Adam Sandler or the creators of Epic Movie and its ilk. These films are lucky to exceed a 10% favourability rating. 

She is excessively disliked by Redditors. I don't enjoy watching basketball, but I don't think people that do are wrong.

Obviously this is US-Centric, but in the UK we have a plethora of female comedians, and never heard a stigma about them not being funny. My favourite comedians are male, but there are still funny women (Sue Perkins pops to mind)

EDIT: Miranda Hart is also up there
